doenjang
Doenjang is fermented Korean soybean paste
tempeh
Tempeh is a fermented whole-soybean product
miso
Miso is a fermented soybean paste
ssamjang
Condiment blends fermented soybean paste (doenjang) with chili paste
pad see ew
"See ew" is Thai for soy sauce, the dish's defining seasoning
sukiyaki
Simmering broth (warishita) is soy sauce, mirin, and sugar
teriyaki
Classic teriyaki glaze is soy sauce, mirin/sake, and sugar
bulgogi
Korean bulgogi marinade is soy sauce, garlic, sugar, sesame oil
gyudon
Beef simmered in a soy sauce-dashi-mirin broth
general tso
Sweet-savory sauce base is soy sauce
kalbi
Korean short rib marinade is soy sauce-based
katsudon
Egg and pork simmered in soy sauce-dashi sauce
oyakodon
Chicken and egg simmered in soy sauce-dashi broth
unagi
Grilled eel is glazed in kabayaki sauce, which is soy sauce-based
char siu
Marinade traditionally includes hoisin sauce and soy sauce
nasi goreng
Indonesian fried rice seasoned/colored with kecap manis (sweet soy sauce)
kung pao
Sichuan stir-fry sauce is built on soy sauce
mee goreng
Fried noodles typically seasoned with soy sauce
moo shu
Traditionally served with hoisin sauce, which is soybean-based
chow mein
Stir-fried noodles commonly seasoned with soy sauce
lo mein
Noodles tossed in a soy sauce-based seasoning
orange chicken
Sticky glaze typically includes soy sauce
yakiniku
Served with tare dipping sauce made from soy sauce
bibimbap
Seasoned namul vegetables are typically dressed with soy sauce and sesame oil
yakitori
Tare-glazed skewers use a soy sauce base, though shio (salt-only) versions are also common
dan dan
Sauce commonly includes soy sauce alongside doubanjiang and sesame paste
california roll
Imitation crab (surimi) filler often contains soy protein/starch as a binder
